title: The Iron Cradle
description: You wake in a dungeon cell with no memory of your crime. The guards are gone, the torches are dying, and somewhere below the floor something is digging upward.
author: AI

intro.passage: You are lying on cold stone. Your wrists ache from old shackles now hanging loose. The cell door stands ajar and the corridor beyond is dark. A rhythmic scraping echoes from beneath the floor. The last torch on the wall gutters, throwing shadows that move like fingers.

torch-corridor.passage: You pull the torch free and step into the corridor. Iron doors line both walls, all sealed shut. At the far end, a staircase spirals upward. Halfway down the hall, a grate in the floor has been pried loose. Warm air rises from the opening and carries a smell like wet copper.

floor-sound.passage: You press your ear to the stone. The scraping is closer now, deliberate and patient. Between strokes you hear breathing - something large and slow. A crack splits the flagstone beneath your knee. Dust puffs upward. Whatever is below, it is almost through.

dark-corridor.passage: Without light the corridor is a throat of black stone. You trail one hand along the wall and count doors. Seven on the left, seven on the right, all locked. Your fingers find a gap where mortar has crumbled. Behind the wall you hear wind - an outside wall. The staircase is somewhere ahead.

creature-breach.passage: The floor splits open. A pale, eyeless mole the size of a horse pushes through the rubble. Its claws are iron-hard and caked with clay. It turns its blind head toward you, nostrils flaring. It does not attack. Instead it pushes a bundle of cloth toward your feet with its snout.

bundle-key.passage: Inside the cloth you find a brass key and a folded note. The note reads: 'The warden buried what he stole beneath the east tower. This key opens the ossuary gate. Trust the digger.' The mole waits, head tilted, as if it understands. It turns and crawls back into the hole.

wall-gap.passage: You claw at loose mortar until a stone falls outward into empty air. Moonlight floods through the gap. You are three stories above a courtyard. Below, a hay cart sits beside the stable. The drop is survivable but ugly. Voices echo from the stairwell behind you - someone is coming.

guard-room.passage: The staircase opens into a ransacked guard room. Tables are overturned and a cold meal rots on a plate. The main door is barred from inside. A ring of keys hangs on a peg beside a lantern that still holds oil. Through the arrow slit you see the courtyard and the east tower beyond.

tunnels.passage: The tunnel smells of earth and iron. Mole tracks score the clay floor in both directions. To the east the passage slopes downward toward distant dripping sounds. To the west it rises and you feel a draft of fresh air. Scratch marks on the ceiling glow faintly with pale fungus.

equipped-hall.passage: Lantern lit and keys jangling, you push into the main corridor. It stretches toward the east tower. Cell doors stand open everywhere - every prisoner is gone. On the floor you find a warden's badge snapped in half and a smear of dried blood leading through a door marked 'Ossuary'.

main-hall.passage: You lift the heavy bar and the door groans open. A long stone hall stretches ahead, lit by moonlight from high windows. Footprints in the dust lead in two directions - east toward the tower and south toward a chapel. The silence here is deeper than in the cells. Too deep.

warden-journal.passage: Beneath the overturned table you find a leather journal. The last entry reads: 'They are asking about the relic again. I moved it to the ossuary - behind the third skull on the north wall. If they come for me I will use the east tower passage. Gods forgive what I have done.'

courtyard-drop.passage: You hit the hay hard enough to drive the breath from your lungs. Straw clouds the air around you. The courtyard is empty but the main gate is chained shut. The east tower looms above you, its door cracked open. Beyond the wall you hear a river. Freedom is close.

choices.label: climb the wall toward the river

ambush-guards.passage: You press into a doorway as two figures pass. They are not guards. They wear black hoods and carry the warden's strongbox between them. One whispers about a boat waiting at the river dock. They vanish up the stairs without checking the cells. Thieves, not jailers.

ossuary-gate.passage: A rusted iron gate blocks the passage. Skulls are stacked floor to ceiling beyond it. If you have a key or strong hands, you might get through. The air here is thick with dust and something older. On the third skull from the left on the north wall, a faint rune glows blue.

east-tower-door.passage: The east tower door opens onto a spiral stair climbing into darkness. Halfway up, a landing holds a heavy chest bound in iron. The lock is smashed open and the chest is empty save for a velvet impression where something round once sat. Above, the stair continues to the roof.

chapel.passage: The chapel pews have been shoved aside to clear a circle on the floor. Chalk symbols cover the stone. Candle stubs ring the circle, recently burned. In the center sits a clay bowl holding dark liquid. Whatever ritual happened here, it was not holy. A side door leads outside.

ossuary-relic.passage: Behind the glowing skull you find a hollow space. Inside rests a crystal sphere no bigger than your fist. It pulses with cold light and hums when you touch it. The warden stole this from someone - or something. With it in hand you feel the walls of the prison thin around you.

tower-roof.passage: Wind hits you on the tower roof. The river gleams below the eastern wall. A rope is tied to the stone battlement - someone planned this exit. The hooded thieves are already at the dock, loading a boat. You could slide down the rope and reach the riverbank before they cast off.

secret-passage.passage: A false panel in the chest base swings open to reveal a narrow chute dropping into darkness. Cool air flows upward. You hear the river clearly now. The warden's escape route - built for exactly this kind of night. It smells like freedom and damp stone.

ritual-knowledge.passage: The symbols are a binding circle meant to hold something pulled from the earth. The bowl holds not blood but groundwater mixed with crushed iron - a ward against burrowing creatures. Someone called the mole here and then tried to cage it. The side door still hangs open behind you.

wall-climb.passage: You scale the outer wall with bleeding fingers. The river rushes below, dark and fast. You drop into the current and the cold seizes your chest. But the water carries you downstream, away from the prison, away from whatever happened there. You do not have answers. You have your life.

ending.title: Cold Current
ending.flavor: The river does not care who you were. It only carries you forward.

relic-escape.passage: The crystal sphere lights your way through the tunnels like a captive star. You emerge on the riverbank under a sky thick with stars. The sphere hums against your chest and you feel its pull - eastward, toward whoever made it. You are free, but the relic has plans of its own.

ending.title: The Sphere's Keeper
ending.flavor: Some treasures choose their bearer, not the other way around.

river-escape.passage: You hit the riverbank running and do not stop until the prison is a dark shape on the horizon. Dawn finds you on a trade road with nothing but the clothes on your back. A farmer's cart rattles past and the driver offers you a seat. The world is wide and you are free.

ending.title: The Open Dawn
ending.flavor: Freedom is a dirt road and a stranger's kindness.

thief-confrontation.passage: You drop to the dock and block their path. The thieves freeze. One draws a blade but the other raises a hand. 'We are not your enemy,' she says. 'The warden locked us all up for the same reason - we found what he was hiding.' She offers her hand. 'Come with us. Do not stay.'

join-ending.passage: You take her hand and step into the boat. The current pulls you south through the gorge. The other passengers are scholars, merchants, a healer - all imprisoned by the warden to protect his secret. As the prison disappears behind the cliff, someone begins to laugh. Then everyone does.

ending.title: Fellow Prisoners
ending.flavor: The best allies are the ones who survived the same cage.
